LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT RESTAURANT DEPOT, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT. MATTER CALLED. MR. BUBION ADDRESSES THE REASONS FOR HIS UNTIMELY NOTICE OF HIS INTENT TO CONTEST THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT PASSED THE MATTER FOR MR. BUBION TO ATTEMPT TO CONTACT OPPOSING COUNSEL. AT THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER, MR. BUBION INFORMED THE COURT THAT HE WAS UNABLE TO REACH OPPOSING COUNSEL. THE COURT INFORMED MR. BUBION THAT HE DID NOT TIMELY CONTEST THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND ON THAT BASIS AS WELL AS THE UPCOMING TRIAL DATE THAT IT IS ADOPTING THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RESTAURANT DEPOT, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST PROPOSED NEW PARTY JINRONG LI DBA PLAZA GARIBALDY AND ASSERTING CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT AND EXPRESS INDEMNITY IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER FILED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Continued To Jul-26-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Restaurant Depot, Llc'S Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint [Per Ex Parte Order Filed 3/8/2021]
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Justin A. Bobion, Esq. of Traub Lieberman Straus & Shrewsberry LLP, 213.624.4500, for Moving Party/Defendant Restaurant Depot, LLC.;
No other appearance by any party or counsel.

Defendant Restaurant Depot, LLC's Motion For Leave To File A Cross-Complaint.

Matter called. Mr. Bubion addresses the reason for his untimely notice of his intent to contest the Court's tentatiive ruling. The matter is passed for Mr. Bubion to contact opposing counsel.

At the second call of the matter, Mr. Bubion informed the Court that he was unable to reach opposing counsel. Brief DISCUSSIONS are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court informed Mr. Bubion that he did not timely contest the Court's tentative ruling and on that basis as well as the upcoming trial date that it is adopting the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Restaurant Depot, LLC's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint against proposed new party Jinrong Li dba Plaza Garibaldy and asserting causes of action for breach of written contract and express indemnity is denied. A party must obtain leave of court to file a cross-complaint after a date for trial is set, and the court may grant leave "in the interest of justice." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 428.50(c).) This action was filed on December 9, 2019, and is set for trial on April 12, 2021. Plaintiff shows that on December 10, 2017, the date of the underlying incident, Defendant had knowledge of Ms. Li, Plaintiff's wife, and of the facts on which the proposed cross-complaint is based. Defendant has unduly delayed in seeking leave to file its cross-complaint, and Plaintiff has shown that he would be prejudiced if the motion were granted due to the need to reopen discovery and to continue the trial date. Further, any action for indemnity could be filed after the underlying action is resolved.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-30-2020 Set For Trial On Apr-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
